sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Imunon Expands At-The-Market Equity Offering Capacity
feed.summary: Imunon (IMNN) has expanded its at-the-market (ATM) equity offering capacity to $17 million by registering an additional $7 million of common stock with H.C. Wainwright & Co. This move indicates the company's ongoing reliance on public equity markets for funding, which could lead to future shareholder dilution. While an analyst has a Buy rating on IMNN with a $25.00 price target, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it Neutral due to weak financial performance and technical downtrends, despite positive earnings call updates.

feed.title: Pacific Biosciences of California researcher uncovers genetic variants shaping brain disorders
feed.summary: Pacific Biosciences of California is exploring the genetic basis of rare ataxias to understand neurological function and disorders better. By focusing on small communities, the company's research aims to identify subtle genetic variants and population patterns to advance personalized medicine and neurological research. This initiative builds on their recent developments in HiFi sequencing technology for detailed cancer variation analysis and automatic epigenetic detection.

feed.title: Fathom Holdings Adds Subordinated Bridge Note Financing
feed.summary: Fathom Holdings (FTHM) secured a $2 million subordinated secured bridge promissory note on March 18, 2026, with an April 1, 2027 maturity and 9% interest. While this financing boosts short-term liquidity, it introduces tighter operating constraints, increased leverage, and stronger creditor control due to extensive covenants and a secured lien on assets. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates FTHM as Neutral, citing weak financial quality and challenging valuation despite encouraging growth in the latest earnings call.

feed.title: Oil Prices Dive. Trump Says Iran Has Agreed to No Nuclear Weapons.
feed.summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth believes energy markets are not accurately pricing the oil supply shock caused by the ongoing war in Iran. Speaking at the CERAWeek conference, Wirth indicated that crude futures should be higher given the significant supply reduction and extensive infrastructure damage. A record number of attendees are present at this year's annual gathering in Houston.

feed.title: Global Payments Drop More Than 28% in One Year: What’s the Best Way to Approach This Stock?
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (GPN) has seen its stock drop over 28% in the past year, underperforming its sector and the S&P 500, due to concerns about fintech, acquisitions, and growth expectations. The company faces challenges like low return on invested capital, high debt, and increased competition, leading to a low valuation. A potential recovery hinges on strong execution, successful integration of acquisitions, improved profitability, and expanded strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Blackstone plans to invest $15B in Japanese property over three years - report (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) intends to invest $15 billion in Japan's property market over the next three years. The private equity firm plans to acquire various types of properties including hotels, data centers, and logistics facilities. This move reflects Blackstone's strategy to expand its real estate portfolio in key international markets.

feed.title: Ross Stores (ROST) operations chief granted 8,579 shares, 2,802 withheld
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. President of Operations, Stephen C. Brinkley, was granted 8,579 shares of common stock as a performance award on March 20, 2026. A portion of these shares, 2,802, were immediately disposed of at $211.19 per share to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, Brinkley directly holds 68,897 shares of Ross Stores common stock, with the granted shares vesting over three years.

feed.title: Robert Fishman Believes that The Walt Disney Company (DIS) Stock Needs Increased Confidence, Reports Bloomberg
feed.summary: A Bloomberg report, citing media analysts Robert Fishman and Rich Greenfield, suggests that The Walt Disney Company's stock needs increased investor confidence, particularly in its streaming growth and improved profit margins. The article highlights the challenges for Josh D'Amaro, Bob Iger's successor, in strengthening Disney's traditional media business and streaming subscriber growth amidst price hikes and declining traditional TV viewership. Greenfield further suggests separating ESPN and ABC networks to unlock shareholder value.

feed.title: AES website adds parent liquidity schedules with quarterly updates
feed.summary: The AES Corporation announced on March 24, 2026, the posting of new fixed income investor materials, "Parent Liquidity Schedules," on its website. This document, available under Investors > Fixed Income Resources & Debt Summary, will be updated quarterly to provide detailed information for bondholders. The release emphasizes transparency regarding the company’s liquidity and debt structure, following recent activities related to consent solicitations and a pending $15.00 per share cash merger.

feed.title: KB Home stock dips after Q1 earnings disappoint and guidance fails to buoy spirits
feed.summary: KB Home's stock declined following its first-quarter earnings report, which disappointed investors. Despite the earnings miss, the company's guidance for the upcoming period also failed to reassure the market, leading to a dip in share price.

feed.title: Ball Corp. stock faces pressure amid aluminum price volatility and beverage can demand shifts
feed.summary: Ball Corp. is experiencing pressure due to rising aluminum prices and moderating demand in the beverage industry, despite its strong position in sustainable aluminum packaging. The company's recent earnings were impacted by higher raw material costs, though management remains confident in future volume recovery and is implementing strategies like supply chain optimization and recycling investments. Strategic expansions and diversification into aerospace also support its long-term outlook, while US investors benefit from its strong domestic market presence and commitment to sustainability.

feed.title: Apollo Global Mourns Director David Simon’s Passing
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management announced the passing of independent director David Simon on March 22, 2026. Simon had served on the company's board since June 2021, and his death creates a vacancy that will require the company to review its board structure and governance. Analysts currently rate APO stock as a "Buy" with a $165.00 price target, and Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates it as "Outperform" due to solid financial performance and a bullish earnings outlook.

feed.title: John Dorfman: Defense stocks could explode upward again
feed.summary: John Dorfman predicts that military spending will increase in 2027 and 2028 due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, recommending investors include defense stocks in their portfolios. He provides a rundown of major U.S. and European defense contractors, highlighting their profitability, debt levels, and stock valuations. Dorfman also reviews his previous defense stock recommendations, noting their strong performance against the S&P 500.

feed.title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Finance Corp. is offering $41,116,000 in Contingent Income Auto-Callable Securities linked to NVIDIA Corporation's stock, maturing in March 2029. These principal-at-risk securities offer quarterly contingent coupons if NVIDIA's stock price meets certain thresholds, with an automatic call feature if the stock price equals or exceeds the initial price. Investors risk losing a significant portion or all of their principal and do not participate in stock appreciation.

feed.title: Coca-Cola FEMSA Shareholders Approve 2025 Results and 2026 Dividend at Annual Meeting
feed.summary: Coca-Cola FEMSA's shareholders have approved the company's 2025 financial results and the composition of its governance bodies for 2026. They also authorized a cash dividend of Ps. 0.9675 per share, or Ps. 7.74 per KOF UBL unit, to be paid in four quarterly installments throughout 2026. Despite solid operating fundamentals, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates KOF as Neutral due to cash-flow deterioration in 2025 and potential risks for 2026.

feed.title: FedEx launches two-hour and end-of-day delivery options
feed.summary: FedEx has launched a new expedited parcel service called "FedEx SameDay Local" offering two-hour and end-of-day delivery options, in collaboration with OneRail. This move comes shortly after Amazon introduced its own faster delivery options, including 1-hour and 3-hour services in select cities. The new FedEx service aims to provide consumers with more flexible delivery choices to meet varying needs, from immediate arrivals to more economical options.

feed.title: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) CFO withholds 204 shares for taxes
feed.summary: Stryker's CFO, Preston Wendell, reported a routine tax-related disposition where 204 shares of common stock were withheld to cover tax obligations. This non-market transaction was valued at $335.67 per share. Following this, Wendell directly holds 6,498 shares and indirectly holds 288 shares through a 401(k) plan, including shares from the ESPP and PIA plans.

feed.title: Aptiv plc stock faces uncertainty amid EV slowdown and ADAS hiring signals in key US markets
feed.summary: Aptiv plc continues to show strong hiring activity in Michigan for ADAS engineering roles despite a broader slowdown in the EV sector. This sustained investment in talent suggests Aptiv is committed to its core competencies in advanced driver assistance systems and electrification solutions. For US investors, Aptiv represents a resilient player in the automotive technology sector, balancing near-term market volatility with long-term growth potential in software-defined vehicles and autonomous driving.

feed.title: PulteGroup Inc. stock faces headwinds amid cooling US housing demand and rising inventory pressures
feed.summary: PulteGroup Inc. is facing significant challenges in early 2026 due to cooling US housing demand, rising inventory, and persistent high mortgage rates. The company experienced a 12% year-over-year drop in new orders and increased unsold inventory, pushing down margins. Despite some operational efficiencies and an attractive valuation, the housing market headwinds, coupled with economic uncertainty, prompt caution for investors.

feed.title: Several sue Stryker after cyberattack, alleging company failed to protect sensitive data
feed.summary: Multiple lawsuits have been filed against Stryker Corporation, including one by a current employee, following a cyberattack. The lawsuits allege that the medical technology company failed to adequately protect sensitive personal information, raising questions about the attack's scope, the effectiveness of Stryker's cybersecurity, and the duration it went undetected. While Stryker claims the breach is contained, experts suggest that residual threats could remain, and personal data could be exploited or sold.

feed.title: Bank of America (BAC) launches 5.20% fixed callable notes due 2036
feed.summary: Bank of America Corporation is offering fixed-rate callable notes due March 27, 2036, with an annual interest rate of 5.20%, payable semi-annually. The notes are senior, unsecured obligations and are callable by Bank of America starting March 27, 2031. They are issued in minimum denominations of $1,000 and are not FDIC insured or bank guaranteed, making them subject to Bank of America's credit risk.

feed.title: Capital One: 3 ways to lean into AI responsibly
feed.summary: Sanjiv Yajnik, president of financial services at Capital One, highlights three key areas for auto lenders responsibly adopting AI: aligning technology with regulations, addressing potential issues with AI-driven information sharing, and ensuring innovation keeps pace with industry changes. Capital One is focusing on integrating AI into auto finance for both lending and broader applications. The article emphasizes the importance of these considerations as AI continues to transform the automotive industry.

feed.title: Expeditors International stock faces logistics sector pressures amid global supply chain shifts and
feed.summary: Expeditors International (EXPD) is navigating challenges in the global logistics sector due to softening demand, fluctuating freight volumes, and geopolitical trade tensions. The company's asset-light model, strong cash flow, and technological investments offer stability, particularly in its air freight export business, but investors need to watch for margin compression and competitive pressures. US investors will find EXPD appealing for its exposure to global trade recovery, strong balance sheet, and dividend growth potential, despite risks from inflation, labor shortages, and potential recession.

feed.title: Two Harbors bidding war heats up as third company competes with UWM, CrossCountry offers
feed.summary: Two Harbors Investment Corp. is embroiled in a bidding war, receiving a third acquisition offer that complicates its original merger agreement with UWM Holdings Corp. This new, undisclosed bidder has made a cash offer of $10.75 per share, surpassing CrossCountry Mortgage's previous bid of $10.70 per share, both of which include a $25.4 million termination fee to UWM. While UWM and Two Harbors are discussing revised terms, the ongoing competition has led to a postponement of the special stockholders meeting until April 7.

feed.title: Stryker says malware was involved in recent cyberattack as production lines reopen
feed.summary: Medical device firm Stryker is restarting production lines two weeks after a cyberattack, allegedly by Iranian actors, wiped data from over 200,000 company devices. While the company initially denied malware involvement, a recent update confirmed that a malicious file was used to run commands and hide attacker activity. Stryker, with assistance from Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42, is restoring systems and assures customers that their technology was not compromised, despite the attack impacting internal corporate Microsoft systems and temporarily affecting emergency medical care in some Maryland hospitals.
feed.source: The Record from Recorded Future News
feed.source_domain: The Record from Recorded Future News

feed.title: IDEX Corporation stock faces uncertainty amid quiet trading and institutional shifts as industrials
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation stock is experiencing quiet trading on the NYSE, with institutional shifts like Nordea Investment Management AB trimming its stake, signaling caution. US investors are advised to monitor Q1 earnings amidst stabilizing demand in fluidics and fire safety within the industrial cycle. The company's core business in fluid technologies and health & science remains strong, offering defensive stability and dividend reliability, while sector dynamics and potential risks like competition and regulatory shifts require close attention.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) affiliate files resale of 1,667 shares reported
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE: KEYS) reported an affiliate sale filing for 1,667 shares of common stock. The filing details two restricted-stock vesting lots from November 2022 and a past sale of 1,667 shares on March 19, 2026, for $483,430.00. An insider transactions specialist assessed the filing as a routine resale of vested compensation shares, with a neutral impact and sentiment.

feed.title: Kelly Services (KELYA) CEO adds 10,100 shares in open-market buys
feed.summary: Christopher D. Layden, President and CEO of Kelly Services (KELYA), purchased 10,000 shares of Class A common stock at $8.7561 per share and 100 shares of Class B common stock at $13.5150 per share in open-market transactions. Following these acquisitions, Layden directly owns 382,513 Class A shares and 100 Class B shares. These transactions were reported in a Form 4 filing and indicate a positive sentiment.

feed.title: [144] AMETEK INC/ SEC Filing
feed.summary: This SEC filing provides details for a Form 144 notice of proposed sale of securities for AMETEK Inc. (AME). The filing outlines information about the filer, issuer, and the specific securities to be sold, including the acquisition date and nature of the transaction. A total of 530 common shares with an aggregate market value of $114,893.00 are proposed to be sold on March 24, 2026, through Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.

feed.title: Mary Wilcox to Retire as Solventum Chief Accounting Officer
feed.summary: Mary Wilcox has informed Solventum of her intention to retire and resign as Chief Accounting Officer. Her departure will be effective following the appointment of her successor. This information is based on an SEC filing from March 24, 2026.

feed.title: Medtronic (MDT) cuts FY26 EPS outlook as MiniMed Flex funding and IPO dilute earnings
feed.summary: Medtronic (MDT) has revised its non-GAAP EPS outlook for fiscal year 2026 to $5.50–$5.54, down from the previous range of $5.62–$5.66. This reduction is primarily due to a $157 million one-time charge associated with payments to Blackstone for funding the MiniMed Flex, and an additional $0.04 per share dilution from the recent IPO of 10% of MiniMed. Despite the near-term adjustment, Medtronic maintains its high single-digit EPS growth guidance for fiscal year 2027, indicating that the company views these impacts as temporary rather than structural.

feed.title: Doctors say Pfizer's Lyme disease vaccine trial results 'encouraging' after more than 70% efficacy shown
feed.summary: Pfizer and Valneva's experimental Lyme disease vaccine, PF-07307405, showed over 70% efficacy in late-stage clinical trials, specifically 73.2% efficacy after the fourth dose. While the study missed its primary endpoint due to fewer anticipated cases, re-analysis generated statistically meaningful results, which tick-borne disease experts find "encouraging." The companies plan to seek FDA approval for the vaccine, which is similar to a discontinued 2002 version but is expected to receive stronger endorsement today due to increased awareness of Lyme disease.

feed.title: Home Depot (NYSE: HD) EVP reports tax-related disposition of 239 shares
feed.summary: Michael F. Rowe, EVP at Home Depot, reported a routine tax-related disposition of 239 shares on March 22, 2026. These shares were withheld at $320.75 each to cover tax obligations, and Rowe now directly holds approximately 5,176 shares. This transaction is considered an administrative action rather than a discretionary market trade.

feed.title: CIMG’s Shift to DeFi Relies on Liquid Staking Innovation as Bitcoin Emerges as a Yield-Generating Asset
feed.summary: CIMG Inc., a small-cap company known for co-packing coffee, is undergoing a major strategic shift towards decentralized finance (DeFi) and computing power. This transformation includes increasing its Bitcoin holdings and integrating with liquid staking services through a partnership with Bedrock, aiming to transform Bitcoin into a yield-generating asset. This high-risk, high-reward strategy introduces substantial volatility and capital allocation challenges, necessitating careful monitoring of key milestones like contractual agreements and financial performance.

feed.title: Struggling with Seasonal Energy Bills? Mon Power and Potomac Edison Can Help
feed.summary: Mon Power and Potomac Edison, FirstEnergy companies, are offering bill assistance programs to help customers manage higher energy costs experienced during the winter. These programs include the Dollar Energy Fund, Emergency Assistance Program, and the 2-1-1 Helpline, providing resources for eligible individuals to keep their electricity affordable and avoid service termination. The companies emphasize reaching out early to access available support as spring approaches.

feed.title: BNY high-yield fund sets April cash payout at $0.0175 a share
feed.summary: The BNY Mellon High Yield Strategies Fund (NYSE: DHF) has declared a monthly cash dividend of $0.0175 per share, payable on April 22, 2026. This dividend, sourced from net investment income, is consistent with the previous month's payout. BNY Mellon Investment Adviser, part of BNY Investments, manages the fund and is a significant player in asset management.

feed.title: Updated: Newsmax, Pay TV Groups, Public Interest Groups Sue FCC to Block Nexstar/Tegna Merger
feed.summary: Newsmax, several pay TV groups, and public interest organizations have filed lawsuits and appeals against the FCC's approval of the Nexstar/Tegna merger, despite Nexstar declaring the $6.2 billion acquisition closed. These groups argue the merger is unlawful, will lead to higher consumer prices, and was rushed by the FCC under political pressure, circumventing proper procedures and congressional limits on audience reach. They are seeking to halt the deal and prevent the full integration of operations.

feed.title: Best Buy (NYSE: BBY) executive awarded 23,886 shares, sells 6,336 for taxes
feed.summary: Best Buy senior executive Jason J. Bonfig received a grant of 23,886 restricted shares on March 20, 2026, which will vest in three equal annual installments. On March 23, 2026, Bonfig sold 6,336 shares at an average price of $64.019 to cover tax withholding obligations related to the vesting of restricted shares. Following these transactions, he directly holds 79,137 shares and indirectly holds 4,150.4058 shares in his 401(k) plan.

feed.title: Best Buy (NYSE: BBY) exec granted stock, sells shares for taxes
feed.summary: Best Buy senior executive Kathleen Scarlett received a grant of 21,895 restricted shares, which will vest in three equal annual installments. She subsequently sold 8,049 shares at $64.019 each on March 23, 2026, to cover tax withholding obligations, a non-discretionary transaction. Following these actions, Scarlett directly holds 102,669 shares of Best Buy common stock.

feed.title: Charles Schwab Corp. stock faces pressure amid shifting interest rates and brokerage competition in
feed.summary: Charles Schwab Corp. is navigating challenges from declining net interest margins and client cash outflows due to shifting interest rates. The company is adapting by expanding wealth management services and focusing on recurring fees rather than interest-sensitive income. Despite near-term pressures, its strong franchise and diversified services, including wealth management and retirement accounts, position it for long-term optimism, though risks like deposit migration and cyber threats remain.

feed.title: eBay Adds Tech Marketplace Veteran to Strengthen Board
feed.summary: eBay announced the appointment of Brian H. Sharples, a veteran in the tech marketplace industry, as an independent director to its board, effectively expanding it to 12 members. Sharples, co-founder and former CEO of HomeAway, brings significant experience in building and scaling technology-driven marketplaces. This move aims to leverage his expertise to strengthen eBay's strategic growth, enhance customer focus, and reinforce long-term shareholder value.

feed.title: BNY Mellon High Yield Strategies Fund Declares Dividend
feed.summary: The Board of Trustees of BNY Mellon High Yield Strategies Fund (NYSE: DHF) declared a monthly cash dividend of $0.0175 per share, payable on April 22, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 8, 2026. This dividend amount matches the previous declaration in February. BNY Investments, the fund's adviser, is part of BNY, which manages $2.2 trillion in assets as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: MGM Resorts stock leads casino sector surge amid regulatory bill and Vegas analyst insights
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International's stock is experiencing a surge due to a surprise regulatory bill boosting the casino sector, despite mixed insights from a Truist Securities analyst regarding Vegas trends. The company's diverse operational segments, including Las Vegas Strip Resorts, Regional Operations, and MGM China, along with its digital arm BetMGM, position it to leverage potential policy tailwinds. While near-term Vegas performance shows stagnation, there's cautious optimism for a Q2 recovery, making MGM a focal point for investors tracking leisure recovery.

feed.title: Innodata Expands Revolving Credit Facility to $50 Million With Wells Fargo, Extends to 2029
feed.summary: Innodata has expanded its secured revolving credit facility with Wells Fargo to $50 million, extending the maturity to April 4, 2029. This amendment, entered into on March 19, 2026, aims to support the company's anticipated growth with both new and existing customers. The borrowing base is linked to eligible accounts receivable, which was approximately $30 million as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Victory Capital (VCTR) Pulls Janus Henderson (JHG) Offer, Ending Bidding War
feed.summary: Victory Capital Holdings has withdrawn its nearly $9 billion acquisition proposal for Janus Henderson Group Plc, effectively ending a brief but intense bidding war. This decision follows a revised offer from Trian Fund Management and General Catalyst, which Janus Henderson's board has endorsed and recommended to shareholders.

feed.title: American Airlines elects Mary Dillon to its board of directors
feed.summary: American Airlines Group Inc. has announced the election of Mary Dillon to its board of directors. Dillon, with over four decades of consumer, marketing, and operational experience from major brands like Foot Locker, Ulta Beauty, and U.S. Cellular, will serve on the Compensation Committee and Corporate Governance and Public Responsibility Committee. The airline's leadership, including Chairman Greg Smith and CEO Robert Isom, highlighted her strategic vision, transformation leadership, and extensive expertise as valuable assets to the company.

feed.title: Globe Life Inc. stock faces scrutiny amid insurance sector pressures and market volatility
feed.summary: Globe Life Inc., a significant player in life and supplemental health insurance, is under investor scrutiny due to rising claims costs, competitive dynamics, and market volatility. Despite steady policy growth and a strong direct-to-consumer model, the company faces headwinds from higher morbidity rates and low interest rates impacting investment income. Investors are closely monitoring its resilience, capital discipline, and ability to adapt to changing market conditions and regulatory environments.

feed.title: Why NetApp (NTAP) Stock Is Trading Up Today
feed.summary: NetApp (NTAP) shares rose 3.1% today after the company announced a strategic alliance with Commvault for integrated data protection and cyber resilience, and unveiled new EF-Series storage systems with 250% performance improvement. These developments are seen as meaningful by the market, though not fundamentally changing its perception of the business. The stock's current performance comes amidst broader market volatility, particularly due to geopolitical developments in the Middle East, and despite a 2% year-to-date decline for NetApp.

feed.title: Kyndryl (KD) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl (KD) fell over 5% after Morgan Stanley slashed its price target and Guggenheim downgraded its rating from Buy to Neutral due to key management departures. This recent downturn follows earlier disclosures by Kyndryl about materially misstated financial statements and inadequate internal controls, along with an SEC inquiry into its cash management practices. The stock has been highly volatile, remaining significantly below its 52-week high, and investors who bought at the IPO have seen a substantial decrease in value.

feed.title: KB Home Stock Sinks After Q1 Revenues Fall 23%
feed.summary: KB Home (NYSE: KBH) reported first-quarter earnings that missed analyst expectations, with revenues falling 23% to $1.08 billion and earnings per share at 52 cents compared to an estimated 58 cents. The company also saw a decrease in homes delivered and average selling price year-over-year. Following the announcement, KB Home's stock slid 4.80% in extended trading.
